# Defect Ledger — Gosling Desktop Workspaces

No finding disappears. Dispositions: fixed, verified-not-a-defect, deferred-with-risk,
blocked-by-missing-input, or blocked-by-tooling. Every fixed defect names a regression test.

Last updated: PC-003

| ID          | Gate   | Source               | Severity | Finding                                                                                   | Root cause                                                                                                    | Disposition           | Patch                                                                               | Regression test                                                                                                                       | Validation evidence                           | Residual risk                                                                   |
| ----------- | ------ | -------------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| DAT-GOS-001 | 4      | data-integrity audit | Medium   | Reused workspace temp file was opened without truncation.                                 | Locking was mistaken for complete atomic-write hygiene.                                                       | fixed                 | truncate-only temp opener                                                           | `stale_longer_temp_is_truncated_before_replacement`                                                                                   | 23-test workspace suite passes                | destructive kill drill not run                                                  |
| DAT-GOS-002 | 4      | data-integrity audit | Medium   | Credential metadata and secure values could partially commit.                             | Two atomic stores lacked a cross-store recovery protocol.                                                     | fixed                 | metadata-first fail-closed writes + pending secure-deletion journal + startup drain | effective-secret status test; store/journal invariants                                                                                | workspace + scoped-secret suites pass         | live keyring failure injection deferred to acceptance/manual runtime            |
| DAT-GOS-003 | 4      | data-integrity audit | Medium   | Incomplete profiles were promoted as configured and produced no workspace warning.        | Metadata presence was treated as readiness.                                                                   | fixed                 | derived actual fields/status + validation warning + prepare-session refusal         | `required_secret_status_is_derived_from_secure_storage_presence`; `unavailable_credential_profile_is_visible_before_session_creation` | workspace suite passes                        | network validation remains provider-dependent                                   |
| DAT-GOS-004 | 4      | data-integrity audit | Medium   | Foreign-platform paths could validate and reach native directory creation.                | Syntax normalization was conflated with native reachability.                                                  | fixed                 | platform-unavailable issues + native-I/O guard                                      | `foreign_platform_primary_folder_blocks_sessions`; `output_creation_rejects_foreign_platform_paths`                                   | workspace suite passes                        | no drive/share translation by design                                            |
| DAT-GOS-005 | 4      | data-integrity audit | Low      | Store reads permitted duplicate canonical IDs/names.                                      | Uniqueness existed only in service writers.                                                                   | fixed                 | read-time identity/schema invariants + corruption quarantine                        | `duplicate_workspace_identity_is_quarantined_on_startup`                                                                              | workspace suite passes                        | ambiguous records are preserved for manual recovery, not merged                 |
| WFG-GOS-001 | 5      | workflow/GUI audit   | Medium   | Folder chooser/reveal/output creation could fail silently.                                | Fire-and-forget async paths lacked result checks.                                                             | fixed                 | result-derived actionable feedback                                                  | focused workspace component tests                                                                                                     | full Desktop suite passes                     | Electron shell behavior mocked in jsdom                                         |
| WFG-GOS-002 | 5      | workflow/GUI audit   | High     | Secret-shaped provider errors could render verbatim.                                      | General error formatting did not redact renderer output.                                                      | fixed                 | bounded workspace error redaction + form clearing                                   | credential sentinel failure test                                                                                                      | full Desktop suite passes                     | unknown secret formats require Gate 6 review                                    |
| WFG-GOS-003 | 5      | workflow/GUI audit   | Medium   | Optimistic session rows dropped pinned workspace identity.                                | One session projection omitted new fields.                                                                    | fixed                 | complete optimistic projection                                                      | `sessionToListItem` workspace regression                                                                                              | full Desktop suite passes                     | none known                                                                      |
| WFG-GOS-004 | 5      | workflow/GUI audit   | High     | Unrelated refresh reset an intentional temporary working directory.                       | Object identity was treated as a workspace change.                                                            | fixed                 | ID/previous-primary reconciliation                                                  | `reconcileWorkspaceWorkingDir` switch/edit/override tests                                                                             | full Desktop suite passes                     | temporary override intentionally not restart-persistent                         |
| WFG-GOS-005 | 5      | workflow/GUI audit   | Low      | Warning indicator hid the actionable validation reason.                                   | Structured issues were collapsed to a boolean.                                                                | fixed                 | warning title and accessible name include messages                                  | sidebar warning accessibility test                                                                                                    | full Desktop suite passes                     | dense lists may truncate visual title; accessible name remains                  |
| WFG-GOS-006 | 5      | workflow/GUI audit   | Low      | Removing the default output left an invalid draft.                                        | Dependent default invariant was not transferred.                                                              | fixed                 | promote first remaining output                                                      | default-output removal regression                                                                                                     | full Desktop suite passes                     | explicit reordering out of scope                                                |
| WFG-GOS-007 | 5      | workflow/GUI audit   | Medium   | Extension always-allow falsely reported a consumed live request as stale.                 | A consuming liveness gate was followed by an impossible check.                                                | fixed                 | retain consuming resolve as the sole gate                                           | ToolApprovalButtons live/stale ordering tests                                                                                         | full Desktop suite passes                     | unrelated pre-existing regression surfaced by acceptance suite                  |
| SEC-GOS-001 | 6      | security audit       | High     | ACP debug mode logged credential-profile request values.                                  | Generic ACP debug logging lacked structured redaction.                                                        | fixed                 | recursive ACP diagnostic redaction                                                  | `http-stream-redaction.test.ts`                                                                                                       | SDK build/test/typecheck pass                 | unknown arbitrary secret formats outside declared fields cannot be inferred     |
| SEC-GOS-002 | 6      | security audit       | Medium   | Flattened future workspace fields could preserve secret-shaped data.                      | Forward-compatibility map bypassed the secret-field guard.                                                    | fixed                 | validate unknown fields with canonical secret-shape rejection                       | `secret_shaped_unknown_fields_are_never_persisted`                                                                                    | workspace suite passes                        | externally edited corrupt backups remain owner-only for manual recovery         |
| CON-GOS-001 | 6      | concurrency audit    | High     | Concurrent profile writes could lose or misalign secure state.                            | Cached aggregate RMW and profile protocol used process-local locks.                                           | fixed                 | cross-process fresh-read secret lock + credential transaction lock                  | concurrent Config and workspace-store writer tests                                                                                    | focused concurrency/workspace pass            | destructive process-kill drill not run                                          |
| LLM-GOS-001 | 6      | LLM security audit   | Medium   | Workspace metadata entered system context as instruction-like prose.                      | User/config data and operational guidance shared one prose block.                                             | fixed                 | typed JSON data boundary; retain deterministic backend/tool controls                | `rendered_context_contains_no_credential_metadata`                                                                                    | workspace suite passes                        | model-visible user data remains steerable by nature                             |
| LLM-GOS-002 | 6      | LLM security audit   | Medium   | Workspace metadata could exhaust the session context.                                     | No canonical collection, field, or aggregate size limits.                                                     | fixed                 | count/length limits plus 64 KiB serialized boundary                                 | `workspace_boundary_limits_model_context_size`                                                                                        | workspace suite passes                        | limit is product policy and may need future tuning                              |
| ACC-GOS-001 | 8      | acceptance build     | Medium   | Repository-wide build failed in `gosling-cli` because `anyhow::Context` was not imported. | A pre-existing call used the extension trait without bringing it into scope.                                  | fixed                 | import `anyhow::Context` in the review orchestrator                                 | `cargo build`                                                                                                                         | full build passes                             | unrelated to Workspaces but blocked required acceptance                         |
| ACC-GOS-002 | 8      | clippy               | Low      | Session role binding borrowed a copyable `&str`.                                          | New incremental-message persistence retained an unnecessary borrow.                                           | fixed                 | bind the `&str` directly                                                            | full gosling library tests                                                                                                            | clippy and 1,481 tests pass                   | none known                                                                      |
| ACC-GOS-003 | 8      | clippy               | Low      | Distribution credential status contained identical configured branches.                   | Two configured predicates were expressed as separate branches.                                                | fixed                 | combine the configured predicates                                                   | `resolves_only_known_path_placeholders`; workspace suite                                                                              | clippy passes                                 | none known                                                                      |
| ACC-GOS-004 | 8      | clippy               | Medium   | Windows drive-letter normalization indexed a UTF-8 string by byte range.                  | ASCII path syntax made the slice appear safe, but the implementation could panic if its precondition changed. | fixed                 | safe `get_mut(..1)` plus ASCII case conversion                                      | `normalizes_unix_and_windows_paths`                                                                                                   | clippy and workspace suite pass               | Windows behavior is unit-tested cross-platform, not exercised on a Windows host |
| ACC-GOS-005 | 8      | clippy               | Low      | A permission-scope test cloned a path solely to construct a one-item slice.               | Test setup used an unnecessary allocation.                                                                    | fixed                 | `std::slice::from_ref`                                                              | full gosling library tests                                                                                                            | clippy passes                                 | none known                                                                      |
| ACC-GOS-006 | 8      | acceptance test      | Low      | Three Desktop TLS startup tests timed out while a full Rust build ran concurrently.       | CPU/resource contention exceeded the tests' five-second timing budget.                                        | verified-not-a-defect | no product patch; rerun Desktop suite without compiler contention                   | full Desktop suite                                                                                                                    | isolated rerun: 63 files / 479 tests pass     | timing tests remain sensitive on heavily loaded hosts                           |
| IOP-GOS-001 | PC-001 | input/output audit   | Medium   | Malformed base64 could be written and reported as a successful artifact.                  | Permissive decoding was treated as validation.                                                                | fixed                 | strict alphabet/padding/round-trip decoding before write                            | malformed base64 leaves no target file                                                                                                | full Desktop suite passes                     | valid content is not format-inspected                                           |
| IOP-GOS-002 | PC-001 | input/output audit   | Medium   | Simultaneous native downloads could choose the same available filename.                   | Availability considered disk state but not in-flight reservations.                                            | fixed                 | reserve destination until Electron reports `done`                                   | simultaneous download name regression                                                                                                 | full Desktop suite passes                     | separate Gosling processes do not coordinate reservations                       |
| WFG-GOS-008 | PC-001 | workflow/GUI audit   | Medium   | An unroutable native download silently used Electron's fallback behavior.                 | The main-process no-route branch emitted no renderer signal.                                                  | fixed                 | typed failure event plus actionable relink warning                                  | unrouted callback and renderer warning tests                                                                                          | full Desktop suite passes                     | packaged Electron download observed only through source/helper tests            |
| IOP-GOS-003 | PC-001 | input/output audit   | Medium   | In-memory artifact content had no resource bound.                                         | The cross-process save seam accepted arbitrary string length.                                                 | fixed                 | 256 MiB content cap; preserve native file copy                                      | injected-limit rejection before dialog                                                                                                | full Desktop suite passes                     | destructive exhaustion drill not run                                            |
| IOP-GOS-004 | PC-001 | input/output audit   | Low      | Unicode-heavy artifact names could exceed filesystem component byte limits.               | The portable filename cap counted JavaScript characters rather than UTF-8 bytes.                              | fixed                 | code-point-safe 180-byte truncation with bounded extension preservation             | Unicode filename byte-bound regression                                                                                                | full Desktop suite passes                     | native rejection was not exercised on every supported filesystem                |
| CON-GOS-002 | PC-001 | concurrency audit    | Medium   | Older async validation could restore a stale workspace download route.                    | Awaited validators wrote shared per-window state without an ordering guard.                                   | fixed                 | revision-checked routing registry; cleanup invalidates pending writes               | forced out-of-order and clear-during-validation regressions                                                                           | focused registry and full Desktop suites pass | packaged Electron race not observed directly                                    |
| WFG-GOS-009 | PC-002 | workflow/GUI audit   | Low      | A successful model request could erase a provider-list failure.                           | Independent async inventory requests shared and cleared one error state.                                      | fixed                 | separate provider and model inventory error state                                    | provider rejection remains visible while model loading succeeds                                                                        | focused and full Desktop suites pass          | provider inventory still depends on backend availability                        |
| ACC-GOS-007 | PC-002 | clippy               | Low      | The new-session test module preceded a remaining helper item.                              | The scoped test was inserted before the file's final production helper.                                       | fixed                 | move the test module to the end of the module                                        | workspace thinking-effort unit test                                                                                                    | all-target clippy passes                      | none known                                                                      |
| PLY-GOS-003 | PC-003 | application playtest | Medium   | Missing-primary workspaces allowed the composer to accept Send with no actionable start feedback. | Hub submission returned silently for an invalid selected workspace. | fixed | Hub renders the validation issue and passes a submit guard/reason to ChatInput; click and keyboard paths are blocked. | `Hub.test.tsx` invalid-workspace regression | focused Desktop tests, TypeScript, ESLint, and isolated UI replay | none known |
| PLY-GOS-004 | PC-003 | application playtest | Low      | Switching workspaces did not leave a visible future-session indication in the sidebar. | The state was emitted only through an ephemeral toast, which was not reliably observable. | fixed | Sidebar retains an accessible `role=status` notice while preserving the pinned session. | `WorkspaceSidebarSection.test.tsx` pinned-session notice regression | focused Desktop tests, TypeScript, ESLint, and isolated UI replay | notice remains until another switch or view reload |

## Root-cause patterns and prevention rules

| Pattern                                     | Prevention rule adopted                                                                                        | Plan-change ref |
| ------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------- |
| Ambient session inputs (ARC-GOS-002)        | Every workspace-sensitive provider/session input is explicit or scoped and pinned.                             | initial charter |
| Oversized persistence owner (ARC-GOS-001)   | Workspace metadata receives a dedicated store; session manager stores snapshots only.                          | initial charter |
| Scattered artifact egress (PC-001)          | Every Gosling-owned artifact producer uses the shared router; generic save-dialog IPC is not renderer-exposed. | PC-001          |
| Check-then-act download names (IOP-GOS-002) | Native downloads reserve in-flight paths in addition to checking the filesystem.                               | PC-001          |
| Silent output fallback (WFG-GOS-008)        | A route failure crosses the typed IPC boundary and names the operator recovery action.                         | PC-001          |
| Async routing publication (CON-GOS-002)     | Commit validation results only when their per-window revision is still current.                                | PC-001          |
| Character-count filename caps (IOP-GOS-004) | Enforce portable leaf-name budgets in UTF-8 bytes without splitting code points.                               | PC-001          |
| Coupled async error state (WFG-GOS-009)      | Independent inventory requests retain independent loading/error truth.                                         | PC-002          |
